definition: Key price levels from previous sessions that act as primary liquidity references and draw-on-liquidity targets.
depends_on: dealing_range (previous session ranges define these levels)
feeds_into: liquidity (session levels ARE the primary external liquidity)
draw_on_liquidity (nearest unswept session level = TP target)
related_to: CRT_candle_range_theory (previous day candle high/low = PDH/PDL = session levels)
order_pairing (PDH and PDL are a PAIRED set — sweep one, target the other)
bridge_integration: Session levels (PDH/PDL/PWH/PWL/PMH/PML/Asian range/key opens) are produced upstream by analysis.liquidity.build_liquidity_map and consumed at five points. (1) Detection: bridge/ict_pipeline.py:522 calls build_liquidity_map(df_liq, liq_swings) which (per trading-ai-v2/analysis/liquidity.py:219-328) emits LiquidityLevel objects with source ∈ {'PDH','PDL','PWH','PWL','PMH','PML','equal_highs','equal_lows','session_high','session_low','D_OPEN','W_OPEN','M_OPEN','Q_OPEN', plus swing levels}. liquidity_type is BSL for highs and SSL for lows. ict_pipeline.py:577-589 also merges H4 liquidity levels (deduped within 0.1% price proximity) so session levels from the macro TF participate. (2) Sweep significance filter — ict_pipeline.py:532-553: ONLY sweeps of {PDH,PDL,PWH,PWL,PMH,PML,equal_highs,equal_lows,session_high,session_low,D_OPEN,W_OPEN,M_OPEN,Q_OPEN} are tagged 'significant'. Minor swing sweeps are downgraded to 'minor_sweep_only' advanced_factor and do not count toward sweep_detected. The swept-source list is added to advanced_factors as 'sweep_of_<sources>' (e.g. 'sweep_of_PDH+equal_highs'). This implements the card's claim that 'session levels ARE the primary external liquidity'. (3) DOL targeting — ict_pipeline.py:593: get_draw_on_liquidity(levels, current_price, bias=direction) selects the nearest unswept level in trade direction, drawing primarily from these session levels (plus HTF swings). claude_decision.py:657-684 enforces the hard DOL pre-filter: if no fib_tp_level OR key_open is >= 4x ATR away from price, the trade is SKIPped (Grade A/B exempt). (4) Key opens (D/W/M/Q) — ict_pipeline.py:524-527 extracts D_OPEN/W_OPEN/M_OPEN/Q_OPEN to result.key_opens; claude_decision.py:828 prompts Claude that these are 'equilibrium references' and that 'a sweep of the weekly open followed by rejection = high-probability reversal'. (5) Asian range — ict_pipeline.py:744-746: get_asian_range(df_primary) sets SymbolAnalysis.asian_range; claude_decision.py:895-897 prints 'Asian Range: HIGH – LOW (sweep of this range = high-probability entry)'; ict_pipeline.py:835-839 sets above_AsianHigh / below_AsianLow advanced_factors when price has cleared the range in trade direction. (6) Reasoning post-gates — claude_decision.py:1099-1117 _REASONING_HARD_GATE_PHRASES contains explicit phrase matches for 'sweep of pdh', 'sweep of pwh', 'sweep of pmh', 'sweep of asian high', 'sweep of session high' (and the low variants), plus 'sweep of equal highs/lows'; _check_opposing_sweep at line 1149 rejects BUY-after-high-sweep / SELL-after-low-sweep. This is how the order_pairing principle (PDH↔PDL, PWH↔PWL, Asian↔High/Low) is enforced — by blocking trades that fade the implied directional draw. CRT_candle_range_theory (previous-day candle high/low = PDH/PDL framing from this card) is captured implicitly via PDH/PDL detection — there is no separate CRT engine in code.

PDH_PDL.definition: Previous Day High / Previous Day Low
PDH_PDL.significance: Primary intraday liquidity targets. Most common sweep targets during London/NY.
PDH_PDL.order_pairing: PDH and PDL are a PAIR — when price sweeps PDL, PDH becomes the draw on liquidity target (and vice versa)

PWH_PWL.definition: Previous Week High / Previous Week Low
PWH_PWL.significance: Swing trade targets. Weekly PO3: manipulation (Wed) often sweeps one, then distribution targets the other.
PWH_PWL.order_pairing: PWH ↔ PWL are paired. Wednesday manipulation sweeps one; Thursday-Friday distribution targets the other.

PMH_PML.definition: Previous Month High / Previous Month Low
PMH_PML.significance: Macro targets for position trades. When monthly level is swept, expect multi-day directional move toward the opposite.
PMH_PML.connection: PMH/PML interact with IPDA 20/40/60 day ranges. A PMH/PML near an IPDA range extreme = very high significance.

asian_range.definition: Asian session high and low (8 PM - 12 AM ET)
asian_range.significance: London open's primary manipulation targets. Asian range IS the accumulation phase of PO3.
asian_range.connection: Asian range → London sweeps it (manipulation) → NY distributes from the sweep. This IS PO3 applied to sessions.
